hypothesis: If chemically accurate valency representations (aromatic-dependent tables and Kekulized datasets) are used to retrain generative models and their outputs are evaluated with a GFN2-xTB-based geometry protocol, then the generated molecules will show smaller geometric deviations (Δr, Δθ, Δφ) and lower relaxation energies (ΔE_relax) relative to quantum-chemically optimized structures than when evaluated with MMFF94, demonstrating that GFN2-xTB-based evaluation provides a more chemically faithful benchmark.
